traffic.title: Request traffic
traffic.empty: No traffic recorded in this range.
traffic.caption: Request traffic over the selected range

autoDeploy.title: Auto-deploy
autoDeploy.intro: Deploy automatically when your Git host posts to the webhook below and the pushed ref matches one of these rules. With no rules, pushes are received but never deploy.
autoDeploy.secretReveal: Secret — copy it now, it won't be shown again
autoDeploy.secretNone: No secret yet — generate one to enable.
autoDeploy.disableDialogDescription: The current secret is cleared and incoming webhook calls stop deploying until you generate a new secret. Trigger rules are kept.

backups.title: Scheduled backups
backups.emptyDescription: Schedule a dump command for a stateful service — VAC runs it in the container and ships the output to your chosen destination.

dialog.commandHint: Run inside the running container; container env vars (e.g. $POSTGRES_USER) are available.
dialog.secretKeyHint: Leave blank to keep the existing S3 settings & credentials. Re-enter the secret to change any S3 field.

databases.title: Managed databases
databases.emptyDescription: Add a database and VAC provisions it, injects the connection string as an env var, and schedules a nightly backup — no manual config.
databases.confirmRemove: Remove the {{engine}} database "{{name}}"? This drops the data.
databases.injectedNote: Connection string is injected as <0>{{envVar}}</0> — reveal it on the Environment tab.

dialog.title: Add a managed database
dialog.sharedWarning: The first {{name}} database starts a shared instance (~{{footprint}} MB) on this box. Later {{name}} databases reuse it.
dialog.controlPlaneNote: Provisioned inside the shared control-plane Postgres — no extra process.
dialog.envVarHintTaken: This app already binds DATABASE_URL. Leave blank to auto-name (e.g. DATABASE_URL_MARIADB), or set your own uppercase name.
dialog.envVarHint: Leave blank to use DATABASE_URL. Set a custom uppercase name to run multiple databases side by side.
dialog.injectNote: VAC injects the connection string as this env var and schedules a nightly backup. Redeploy the app to pick it up.

deployKey.title: Deploy key
deployKey.description: Add this public key as a read-only deploy key on your Git host so VAC can clone over SSH.

deploys.emptyDescription: Trigger a deploy from your configured branch to get started.
deploys.rollbackDialogDescription: This rebuilds and redeploys commit {{sha}} as a new deployment. Only the code is rolled back — environment variables are left unchanged. The current version keeps serving until the rollback is healthy.

domains.noCustom: No custom domains. This app is reachable at its automatic subdomain.
domains.confirmDelete: Delete {{hostname}}? Its route is removed immediately.
domains.autoTitle: Managed automatically — change the slug or base domain

serviceCard.noBackupTitle: No backup is configured for this service — set one up on the Backups tab.
serviceCard.oomTitle: Killed for exceeding its memory limit
serviceCard.exposedPortHint: Host-published port — diagnostic only, detected from your compose file on each deploy. To change it, edit the service's `ports:`/`expose:` mapping; HTTP services are reached over the internal network, not a host port.
serviceCard.internalPortHint: The port the container actually serves on. Saving re-routes traffic to it immediately — no restart needed.

services.emptyDescription: Services appear here after the first successful deploy.

settings.ramLimitInvalid: RAM limit must be a whole number of MiB (or blank for unlimited)
settings.addonSourceNote: This app was deployed from a curated add-on template. Its repository, build, and auto-deploy settings are managed by the template and can't be edited here.
settings.ramLimitHint: Hard memory ceiling per container — VAC kills it before it can OOM the box. Leave blank for unlimited. Applied on the next deploy.
settings.exportSpecNote: Download this app as a portable <0>vac.app.yaml</0> — build config, services, domains, triggers, and env keys. Nothing here is proprietary: re-import it on another VAC, or keep it in version control for disaster recovery. Sensitive secret values are omitted — you re-enter them on the far side.
settings.dangerNote: Stops all services and permanently removes the app, its containers, and data volumes.
settings.deleteConfirmDescription: This permanently removes the app, its services, configuration, and data volumes — plus any managed database it owns. This action cannot be undone.

shell.title: Shell · {{service}}
shell.description: This session is recorded in the audit log.
shell.warning: Open a root-capable shell into <0>{{service}}</0>? You will have full access inside the container. The session open is audit-logged.

env.restartBanner: Changes saved — restart required to take effect.
env.title: Environment variables
env.emptyState: No variables yet. Use <0>Add variable</0> or <1>Import .env</1>.
env.unsaved_one: {{count}} unsaved change — applied on next restart
env.unsaved_other: {{count}} unsaved changes — applied on next restart
env.about1: Variables are <0>encrypted at rest</0> with the host master key and injected only when containers start. Changes require a restart to take effect.
env.about2: <0>Plain-text</0> values stay visible and editable inline. <1>Write-only</1> values are masked and never shown again — toggle the lock on any row to switch.
env.about3: You can turn a write-only value back to plain-text, but you'll need to type a fresh value: the old one can never be revealed.

import.paste: Paste a <0>.env</0> file. Rows merge into the editor by key; review which values become write-only before importing.
